L1.area: HTTP request handling and XML document processing layer

L2.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability exists where the XML parser used for processing incoming requests does not properly restrict external entity references. An attacker can submit crafted XML payloads containing external entity declarations, resulting in disclosure of confidential server-side data, server-side request forgery (SSRF), port scanning, and denial of service.

L3.area: REST API server and HTTP request processing XML parsers
L3.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability exists in the XML parsers used for processing incoming REST API requests and HTTP configuration. The parsers create XML readers without disabling external entity processing, allowing an attacker to submit crafted XML payloads containing external entity declarations. This can result in disclosure of confidential server-side data, server-side request forgery (SSRF), port scanning, and denial of service.

ground_truth.reason: The REST server's XML parser creates an XMLReader via SAXParserFactory without disabling external entity processing, allowing attackers to submit crafted XML that triggers XXE attacks leading to data disclosure, SSRF, and denial of service.
